Mrs. Charles Fiipot, 1929 Augusta
avenue, wife of the slayer of Nez
Cole, whose sentence to death on
this date was committed by the
governor to life Imprisonment
says that she will soon inaugurate
a movement to secure the com.
plete pardon of her husband. She
says that he Is needed at home to
help raise his boys.


Judge Whitson In the federal
court is hearing arguments in
suit by the government to set aside
title to 100000 acres Of land In the
Yakima Indian reservation, patent
ed to the Northern Pacific in the
early nineties. The history of the
case dates back to treaty made
in 1855, in which the government
the territorial authorities represent
ed by Governor Stevens and Chief
Kamaichan of the Yakimas figured


As part of the harvest festival
now being observed by the Saliva
ton Army, two wagon loads oil
vegetables will be sold at thar
vest auction to be held at the
barracks tonight.


The occasion Of the fourteenth
wedding anniversary Of Mr.. and
Mrs. Robert Turnbull, S425 Hilliar6
street, was observed Friday night
with a surprise party given by Mrs
William Hamilton. There was q
generous outpouring of the Scottish
friends of the Turnbulls, who en-
joyed themselves in the good, old
fashioned way. Mr.. Turnbull was
so surprised and overjoyed at the
gathering that he was unable tC
respond to the calls to dance the
